issues.title: Copilot agents emit add_comment with target="*" but no item_number (NEW — 15 occurrences across 2 workflows)
issues.detail: NEW critical pattern affecting copilot-based workflows. Contribution Check (run 26475827071) had 1 such failure; PR Sous Chef #2 (run 26470389940) had 14. Error: `Target is "*" but no item_number/issue_number/pull_request_number/pr_number/pr/pull_number specified in add_comment item`. The agent is emitting add_comment payloads with target="*" (meaning broadcast/all) but without specifying which issue/PR to comment on. Two paths to fix: (1) update prompt to require item_number when target=*; (2) validate safe-output schema to reject target=* without item_number at MCP boundary.

issues.title: Execute Claude Code CLI step times out at 10min despite successful CLI exit (NEW)
issues.detail: NEW Smoke Claude run 26468168627 — Claude CLI reports terminal_reason=completed and all 19 tests passed (success), but the GitHub Actions step `Execute Claude Code CLI` times out 52s later: `##[error]The action 'Execute Claude Code CLI' has timed out after 10 minutes.` CLI exited at 21:03:24, step error at 21:04:16. Suggests stdout/stderr buffer drain or wrapper cleanup is blocking after CLI exit. Bumping step timeout-minutes from 10 to 12-15 should mitigate. Affected: any Claude workflow whose body+cleanup approaches 10m. Action: check action.yaml for the timeout-minutes on the Claude exec step.

issues.title: Daily Safe Output Tool Optimizer cost jumped to $9.90 (was $6.05 yesterday — +64%)
issues.detail: Run 26475821926 today ran 19.4m, 14.9M tokens, 97 turns, $9.90 — single highest workflow cost we've recorded since the initial $8.54 peak. Trend: $8.54 → $5.55 → ~$5 → $6.05 → $9.90. After plateauing, the cost has now JUMPED past the original peak. Drill into what changed in this workflow (prompt/data inputs) between 05-25 and 05-26. Single workflow now accounts for 38% of daily spend ($9.90 of $26.37).

issues.title: Codex `gpt-5.3-codex` → 404 alpha (down to 1 workflow — others migrated to gpt-5.4)
issues.detail: DOWN from 3 workflows yesterday to 1 today. Only Changeset Generator still uses model `gpt-5.3-codex` in aw_info.json; the CLI sends `gpt-5-codex-alpha-2025-11-07` which 404s at proxy. Successful codex runs today (AI Moderator, Daily Cache Strategy Analyzer, Smoke Codex) all use `gpt-5.4`. Fix: edit Changeset Generator workflow frontmatter to set model: gpt-5.4.

issues.title: awf-squid container unhealthy — RECURRED after appearing resolved
issues.detail: Yesterday I de-escalated this issue (0 occurrences in 174 runs). Today it recurred on PR Sous Chef #1 (run 26475294676): `dependency failed to start: container awf-squid is unhealthy`. Single occurrence today but it definitively wasn't resolved — pattern is intermittent. Squid container health-check is brittle; cause is probably init race with iptables setup. Re-escalating to medium severity for tracking.

issues.title: Smoke Copilot upload_artifact fails: staging directory empty (NEW)
issues.detail: Run 26468168604 — Smoke Copilot test #20 (`upload_artifact: path=gh-aw`) failed with `ERR_VALIDATION: path does not exist in staging directory: gh-aw. The staging directory is empty — did you forget to copy files to /home/runner/work/_temp/gh-aw/safeoutputs/upload-artifacts/?` The agent emitted upload_artifact without first copying files into the staging dir. Either (a) the smoke test's intended `mkdir + cp` step is missing/broken in the prompt, or (b) safe-output should validate staging contents before agent runs. Probably (a) — update smoke prompt to explicitly create test file.

issues.title: PR Sous Chef variance 0-217 turns today (avg 142.8 across 4 runs)
issues.detail: Variance widening — observability surfaced min_turns=0 max_turns=217 today vs 7-65 yesterday. Two failures today both PR Sous Chef (50% failure rate). One failure was awf-squid infra; the other was the add_comment target=* bug. The turn variance might just be 'PR has nothing to do' (0 turns skip) vs 'large PR fanout' (217). Watch.
